text:
Admission Date: 1981-06-20        Discharge Date: 2007-10-12

Date of Birth:  1973-11-22        Sex:  M

Service:  CSU


HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:  Mr. Reed is a 53-year-old
man with no previous history of heart disease.  He had
several months of chest pain and recent positive exercise
tolerance test.  He was admitted to the hospital for cardiac
catheterization, which revealed 95 percent LAD, 60 percent
OM1, 100 percent proximal RCA with an EF of 69 percent.  An
echo done following catheterization showed an EF of 60 to 70
percent with trivial MR Jennifer Marsh dilated aortic root.  He
was referred for coronary artery bypass grafting following
cardiac catheterization.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  Significant for hypertension,
hypercholesterolemia, GERD, and hyperparathyroidism.

MEDS ON ADMISSION:  Univasc, Lipitor which was discontinued
by the patient secondary to fatigue, and aspirin.

He has no known drug allergies.

LABORATORY DATA:  White count 7.6, hematocrit 38.1, platelets
224,000.  INR 1.1, PTT 28.4.  Sodium 136, potassium 3.5,
chloride 105, CO2 23, BUN 14, creatinine 0.9, glucose 127.
LFTs within normal limits.  UA was negative and chest x-ray
showed no active cardiopulmonary disease.

REVIEW OF SYMPTOMS:  Neurologically no CVAs, TIAs, seizures
or headaches.  Pulmonary showed positive  shortness of
breath, no history of COPD.  No asthma.  Cardiac history of
chest pain times several months.  No history of pneumonia or
orthopnea.  GI showed positive GERD, no hemoptysis, no
hematochezia or melena.  GU showed no dysuria.  Endocrine
showed no diabetes or thyroid disease.  Heme showed no
bleeding disorders.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:  Neuro grossly intact.  No carotid
bruits noted.  Pulmonary clear to auscultation bilaterally.
Cardiac regular rate and rhythm, S1 and S2 with no murmur.
Abdomen soft, nontender, nondistended with normoactive bowel
sounds.  Extremities were warm and well perfused with no
edema and no varicosities.  Right groin with ecchymotic area
following catheterization from a bleed.

Following catheterization the patient was followed by the
medical service and on 12-10 he was brought to the
Operating Room.  Please see the OR report for full details.

In summary he had coronary artery bypass grafting times four
with LIMA to the LAD, saphenous vein graft to the diagonal,
saphenous vein graft to the OM and saphenous vein graft to
the RCA.  His bypass time was 71 minutes with a cross clamp
time of 41 minutes.  He tolerated the operation well and was
transferred from the Operating Room to the Cardiothoracic
Intensive Care Unit.  At the time of transfer he was A-paced
at 90 beats per minute with a mean arterial pressure of 72
and a CVP of 9.  He had nitroglycerin 0.1 mcg per kilogram
per minute and propofol 20 mcg per kilogram per minute.

The patient did well in the immediate postoperative period.
His anesthesia was reversed.  He was weaned from the
ventilator and successfully extubated.  The patient remained
hemodynamically stable throughout the operative night and on
postoperative day one remained in the Cardiothoracic
Intensive Care Unit, as he required Neo-Synephrine drip to
maintain an adequate blood pressure.  Throughout the course
of postoperative day one the patient was weaned from his Neo-
Synephrine drip.

By postoperative day two the patient was off all vasoactive
IV medications.  His chest tubes were discontinued as was his
central line and Foley catheter, and he was transferred to
the floor for continued postoperative care and cardiac
rehabilitation.

Over the next several days, the patient had an uneventful
hospital course.  His activity level was advanced with the
assistance of the nursing staff and physical therapy staff.
On postoperative day four it was decided that the patient was
stable and ready to be discharged to home.

At the time of this dictation the patient's physical exam is
as follows:  Temperature 99.8, heart rate 88 sinus rhythm,
blood pressure 128/70, respiratory rate 20, O2 sat 92 percent
on room air.  Patient's weight preoperatively was 76 kg, at
discharge is 81.6 kg.

LABORATORY DATA:  White count 8.9, hematocrit 24.1, platelets
of 186,000.  Sodium 137, potassium 3.9, chloride 100, CO2 30,
BUN 17, creatinine 1.0, glucose 123.  PT 12.7, INR 1.0.

Neuro showed alert and oriented x3, moved all extremities,
nonfocal exam.  Pulmonary clear to auscultation bilaterally.
Cardiac regular rate and rhythm, S1 and S2 with no murmur.
Sternum was stable.  Incision with Steri Strips, clean and
dry with no erythema and no drainage.  Abdomen was soft,
nontender, nondistended with normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities were warm, well perfused with no edema.  Right
saphenous vein graft harvest site with Steri Strips, clean
and dry.  No erythema.

Patient's condition at time of discharge is good.  He is to
be discharged home with visiting nurses.  He is to follow up
in the Kaweah Delta Rehabilitation Hospital Clinic in two weeks.  Follow-up with Dr. Holland
in three to four weeks and follow-up with Dr. Garza in
six weeks.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
1. Potassium chloride 20 mEq every day times two weeks.
2. Colace 100 mg b.i.d. for one month.
3. Aspirin 81 mg every day.
4. Percocet 5/325 one to two tablets every four to six hours
   p.r.n. as needed for pain.
5. Citalopram 20 mg every day.
6. Metoprolol 25 mg b.i.d.
7. Lasix 20 mg every day times two weeks.
8. Ferrous sulfate 325 mg every day times one month.
9.
   Ascorbic acid 500 mg b.i.d. times one month.
10.      Simvastatin 40 mg every day.



                        [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] Wall, 36813
